```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  xv <- 0:4; raw <- sample(1:8,5,replace=TRUE); pr <- raw/sum(raw)
  k <- sample(0:3,1); ans <- sum(pr[xv<=k])
  vals <- round(c(ans,pr[k+1],sum(pr[xv<k]),1-ans,sum(pr[xv>=k])),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals); ptxt <- paste(sprintf("%.3f",pr),collapse=", ")
```

Question
========
Uma variável tem suporte $(0,1,2,3,4)$ e pmf correspondente $(`r ptxt`)$. Qual é $F_X(`r k`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A FDA acumula todas as massas até `r k`:
$$F_X(`r k`)=\sum_{x\leq`r k`}p_X(x)
=`r sprintf("%.4f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S6Q4_FDA_discreta
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
